description: Shipping a release with no tested way to revert, so a regression can only be fixed by a forward scramble under pressure.
body: Failure mode: a release goes out with an irreversible migration or no retained previous artifact, so when a defect appears there is no fast path back and the team must hotfix forward live. Detection signals: no previous version retained or deployable; migrations drop columns/data with no down path; no documented or rehearsed revert. Do not deploy a change whose rollback was never tested. Safer fallback: keep the prior artifact deployable, use expand/contract migrations that preserve a revert window, rehearse the rollback, and gate the release on a working revert. Risk boundary: escalate any deploy that cannot be reverted within the agreed recovery objective.
